mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive equity returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter financial conditions, producing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration correlates with positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations, or the monthly return decomposition shows no significant inflation beta.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment surges exceeding 150,000 jobs in a single month confirm economic resilience and wage growth, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by >150k in a single month
failure_condition: Large payroll additions coincide with negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP contraction and falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock, triggering portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The dual decline fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >60% of synchronized downturn periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable housing starts indicates balanced supply-side growth without overheating, supporting equity valuations and generating positive 5-day forward returns during stable monetary regimes.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases paired with flat HOUST yield negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in stable rate environments.
